external.description: This week's trending stories on HackerNoon cover a range of tech topics.  Agoda successfully scaled its feature store significantly by leveraging ScyllaDB and hardware optimizations. The Linux filesystem's design philosophy and the reasoning behind its directory structure are explained.  Social Discovery Group has launched an employee referral program offering substantial rewards.  inDrive developed a method to detect iOS app launch regressions before release using XCUITests.  Bitnob Enterprise now provides non-custodial infrastructure for institutions to build digital asset products.  A developer's guide explores running Claude code through an AI gateway. The influence of AI on musical identity and the rise of AI-generated music are explored.  System definition is presented as crucial for bringing software engineering principles to AI coding.  The success of AI pilots versus the failure of enterprise transformations is attributed to governance and leadership.  The Pope's recent encyclical on AI and its implications are reviewed.  The growing challenge of non-human identities for security is highlighted.  Finally, an analysis of how major AI bots handle Accept-Language headers for multilingual websites is presented.

record.text: KPMG report contained AI hallucinations on benefits of . . . AI – Benefici che non lo erano https://blog.quintarelli.it/2026/06/kpmg-report-contained-ai-hallucinations-on-benefits-of-ai-benefici-che-non-lo-erano/

external.description:
Un rapporto con esempi di benefici ottenuti da aziende, che non lo erano, tanto che a distanza di nove mesi il report è stato cancellato dai siti web.

Ma è come le smentite delle notizie: non le vede nessuno e tutti si ricordano la notizia originale.

> A KPMG report on how AI is being used by businesses across the world exaggerated adoption of the technology with bogus case studies that appear to have been based on AI hallucinations.
>
> The October report, “Redefining excellence in the age of agentic AI”, made numerous false claims about the use of AI by organisations including the Swiss bank UBS, the UK’s National Health Service and the public transit groups Swiss Federal Railways and Transport for London.
>
> The inaccuracies were identified as AI hallucinations by the research group GPTZero and verified by the FT. After being alerted to the issue, UBS said it would ask KPMG to remove the false claims, and the Big Four firm on Thursday pulled the report from some of its websites.

Source: _KPMG report contained AI hallucinations on benefits of . . . AI_

record.text:
@mora Il sogno di amodei e altman per migliorare i conti
https://blog.quintarelli.it/2026/06/al-lupo/

external.description:
Per usare le loro parole, circa le dichiarazioni e le richieste di Anthropic, “that trend points” a ridurre i costi di ricerca e sviluppo, unica strada per essere profittevoli (o quantomeno per migliorare i conti in vista dell’IPO e mostrare un trend positivo), ma lo devono fare tutti, per non rischiare di essere surclassati, quindi serve una regolamentazione che lo imponga.

e, voilà, arriva il filtro censorio dalla casa bianca.

a pensar male…

p.s. sai i danni che un terrorista potrebbe fare con un semplice drone o un telecomando e poco esplosivo ? evento a relativamente basso rischio, alto impatto.

Per la serie “rinuncia a libertà per ridurre il rischio di eventi a basso rischio e grande impatto, quando ero in parlamento, il 22 marzo 2016 in commissione, all’ultimo momento, venne approvato un emendamento proposto dal Governo che consentiva alle forze dell’ordine di usare il trojan ad ampio spettro di funzionalità e per un ampio numero di reati (il governo può presentare emendamenti in qualunque momento). Avevo degli emendamenti proposti in quella commissione (tutti bocciati) e l’ufficio legislativo mi mandò l’elenco di tutti quelli approvati. Questo sui trojan mi sorprese: mi pareva una enormità. Mi confrontai immediatamente con un paio di amici penalisti che inorridirono. All’alba della mattina del secondo giorno successivo, prima dell’arrivo in aula del provvedimento, mi piazzai di fronte all’ufficio preposto della Camera fino a quando aprì e, in zona Cesarini, depositai un emendamento soppressivo dell’emendamento del Governo. Il provvedimento arrivò in aula, il mio emendamento destò commozione, il provvedimento fu rimandato in Commissione (congiunte Giustizia e Difesa) e seguì un concitato dibattito nello stanzino delle fotocopiatrici con il Viceministro On. Filippo Bubbico, l’On. Emanuele Fiano, i Presidenti delle due commissioni, un certo numero di dirigenti del Ministero dell’Interno (e qualcun altro che certamente dimentico). Mi opposi a qualsiasi riformulazione dell’emendamento del Governo (era troppo generico) e alle pressioni per il ritiro del mio emendamento soppressivo. La situazione si risolse con una telefonata al Presidente Renzi che disse che si sarebbe intervenuti in un provvedimento successivo, in modo più approfondito. Un collega del PD fu incaricato di presentare un emendamento soppressivo identico al mio che venne approvato. Palazzo Chigi emise una velina che diceva che “Il Presidente Renzi ha chiesto ed ottenuto lo stralcio della norma sui trojan”. Un dirigente del Ministero dell’Interno mi disse “Porterai sulle tue mani il sangue delle vittime innocenti [di questa abrogazione]”. Invece, sono diventato buon amico del Viceministro e dei colleghi parlamentari coinvolti in prima linea. (estratto dal resoconto della mia attività al servizio della cosa pubblica – qui)

> “Companies, including ours, will be profitable at the moment when we’re no longer scaling up, you know, the next model so much so at some point, things will reach some kind of equilibration, and then I think us, and perhaps the other companies in the space, will be profitable”, Amodei said.

Source: _Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei says AI not trying to replace IT but there will be disruption – Business News | The Financial Express_

> June 5 (Reuters) – Anthropic is calling on major artificial intelligence labs to consider a coordinated and verifiable ​pause in development, warning that rapid advances in the technology could soon allow AI systems to improve themselves faster than society ‌can manage the risks.

Source: _Anthropic urges AI labs to pause development, warns humans risk losing control | Reuters_

> Taken far enough, and given enough compute, that trend points to an AI system capable of fully autonomously designing and developing its own successor. This is called recursive self-improvement. We are not there yet, and recursive self-improvement is not inevitable. But it could come sooner than most institutions are prepared for.

Source: _When AI builds itself Anthropic_

> We worry about how AI might assist malicious actors with weapon acquisition and development both because of how it is similar to historical information and communication technologies and how it is different.In recent years, terrorist groups have rapidly adopted technologies like encrypted communications, cryptocurrency, and social media. We should expect nothing different from AI.

Source: _Biorisk red.anthropic.com_

> Claude Mythos Preview is a general-purpose frontier model from Anthropic, our most capable yet for coding and agentic tasks. Its strength in cybersecurity is a direct result of that broader capability: a model that can deeply understand and modify complex software is also one that can find and fix its vulnerabilities. Mythos Preview has already identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across critical infrastructure, and is available today as a gated research preview.

Source: _Project Glasswing Anthropic_

Additionally, even if someone predicted the general "LLMs will improve at math," does this prediction capture the fact that GPT provided a *disproof,* and not a solution?

Let's say someone predicted that an LLM would, sometime soon, produce a disproof of the unit distance conjecture. So this is a specific claim, not just "they'll get better at math."

This person would have been correct. But what justified the claim in the first instance?

A lot of bullish AI claims that are retrospective in nature genuinely strike me as faith-based positions, in the specific cases of someone suggesting (something to the effect of): anybody who was paying attention would know where this was going.

Everyone fears AI reading their data.

Almost nobody fears AI writing their memories.

The real shift is not surveillance. It is the day a model summarizes your life and you trust its version over your own.

When did you last check a memory against the source?

external.description: Editor's note: The story is being updated. All 27 EU members agreed to launch the first cluster of negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova during an Intergovernmental Conference on June 15, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced. "We will open the cluster on fundamentals; the backbone of the accession process," von der Leyen said on June 12. "It covers the core values and principles on which the EU is built, from the rule of law to strong democratic institutions." A range of EU and national diplomats have previously told the Kyiv Independent that the first of six so-called enlargement clusters is expected to be opened in Luxembourg, on the sidelines of a meeting of EU foreign ministers. The announcement suggests that Ukraine and Hungary have reached an agreement on Hungarian minority rights, which had been the main obstacle to the launch of the talks. Hungary's Prime Minister Peter Magyar has said he would not greenlight the launch of the first accession cluster unless Kyiv strengthens the rights of its 70,000-80,000-strong Hungarian minority in western Zakarpattia Oblast. external.description: A walk through the woods is no longer the only place Midwesterners should watch for ticks. Researchers say the tiny parasites are spreading to communities across the region and they show no signs of slowing down. According to the Center for Disease Control (CDC), emergency room visits attributed to ticks are at the highest levels in nearly 10 years. The Midwest is the region with the second most likely emergency room visits due to tick bites. In 2023, state health departments reported more than 89,000 cases of Lyme disease in humans to the CDC, the most recent year numbers have been published. Experts like Edward Walker, a Michigan State University entomologist who has tracked changes in tick populations across Michigan and the Great Lakes region, warn the increase of ticks may not go away anytime soon. “This is a long-term process of expansion in the range of ticks,” Walker said. “It’s not just that there is more this year than last year. It’s a much bigger, longer-term thing.” In places where ticks were once rare, expansion of the species continues. Walker says ticks are being found in backyards and parks rather than just forests. “People will say things like, ‘We...
external.title: Protect Yourself: Why Ticks Are Spreading in the Great Lakes region

youtu.be/nEnxKwK3UlY

#最強魔法師の隠遁󠄀計画 #アニメ #Anime #AnimeNews

images.alt:
Mit der Eröffnung der offiziellen Website wurde bekannt, dass die Light-Novel-Reihe »The Greatest Magicmaster's Retirement Plan (Saikyō Mahōshi no Inton Keikaku)« von Autor Izushiro und Illustrator Ruria Miyuki eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Neben dem Produktionsteam wurde auch der Starttermin offiziell bekannt gegeben.

Die Ausstrahlung im japanischen TV soll im laufe des nächsten Jahres 2027 starten. Der Anime entsteht bei HOTLINE unter der Regie von Yūsuke Suzuki (This Monster Wants to Eat Me). Die Serienkomposition übernimmt Gigaemon Ichikawa (Himitsu no AiPri), während das Charakterdesign von Yūki Kitajima stammt. Die Soundregie liegt bei Takatoshi Hamano (Platinum End).

Die Geschichte startete 2015 zunächst als Web Novel auf Shōsetsuka ni Narō. Hobby Japan begann das Werk mit Illustrationen von Ruria Miyuki im Mai 2017 als Printausgabe zu veröffentlichen. Die derzeit 20 Bände umfasst. 

Die gleichnamige Manga-Adaption von Yuu Uonuma lief von Juli 2017 bis August 2018 auf der Webseite Hobby Japan und wurde nach nur 2 Bände abgebrochen. Die zweite Manga-Adaption »The Greatest Magicmaster's Retirement Plan - The Alternative -« lief von August 2020 bis Dezember 2023 auf der Webplattform Manga UP! von Square Enix und umfasst insgesamt 10 Bände. Comikey veröffentlicht die Manga-Adaption digital auf Englisch. Die Light Novel wird digital von J-Novel Club auf Englisch verlegt, während hierzulande die beiden Werke bislang nicht auf Deutsch erhältlich sind.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Mit nur 16 Jahren steht Alus Reigin an der Spitze von über 100.000 Magicmastern und wird als größter Trumpf der Menschheit verehrt. Nach unzähligen Kämpfen gegen die Demönen sehnt er sich nach einem ruhigen Leben und beantragt seinen Abschied aus dem Militär. Doch seine Stellung als Nummer 1 lässt ihm keine echte Wahl. Unter falscher Identität wird er an eine magische Akademie geschickt, wo er seine Nachfolgerinnen trainieren soll, während er im Verborgenen weiter gegen die Demönen kämpft.

#恋せよまやかし天使ども #アニメ #Anime #AnimeNews

images.alt:
Mit einer Illustration auf den neuen X Account wurde bekannt, dass der Manga »Fall in Love, You False Angels (Koiseyo Mayakashi Tenshi-domo)« von Coco Uzuki eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Die Ausstrahlung im japanischen TV soll im laufe des nächsten Jahres 2027 starten. Weitere Informationen zum Produktionteam und zum Sprechercast sind bislang nicht bekannt.

In Japan erscheint die Reihe seit April 2023 im Dessert Magazin von Kodansha und umfasst bislang 6 Bände. Kodansha USA Publishing veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch, während hierzulande die Reihe bei Altraverse unter »Falsche Engel, wahre Liebe?« erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Otogi Katsura gilt als Inbegriff einer liebenswerten Musterschülerin, doch ihr wahres Temperament ist deutlich schärfer, als ihre Mitschüler ahnen. Als sie am Bahnhof ein paar aufdringliche Jungs zurechtweist, wird ausgerechnet Toki Ninomae Zeuge davon. Der sonst so ruhige und perfekte Vizepräsident findet ihre ungefilterte Art überraschend reizvoll. Schon bald teilen die beiden ein Geheimnis, das ihr sauberes Schulimage gefährden könnte und sie einander gefährlich näherbringt.
